fact: March 22 sits exactly 81 days into the year, which means there are still 284 days left to go!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is when maple trees start getting their sap sucked out to make syrup - they're basically tree vampires!
Cats often go absolutely bonkers around this time because spring fever makes them extra playful and they start shedding their thick winter coats everywhere.
The sun rises almost exactly due east and sets due west around March 22, making it super easy to figure out directions without a compass.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, March 22 marks the beginning of autumn when leaves start turning brilliant orange and red.
Earthworms typically start wiggling up to the surface around this date because the soil is finally warming up after winter.
March 22 falls right in the middle of Aries season in the zodiac calendar.
In northern regions, this is when ice on lakes and ponds starts making weird cracking and groaning sounds as it begins to melt and shift.
Dandelions are secretly preparing their yellow invasion around March 22 - their roots are busy storing up energy to explode into bloom soon.
The daylight and darkness are almost perfectly balanced around this time, with each lasting about 12 hours across most of the planet.
In cold climates, March 22 is when you might see the last stubborn patches of snow that refuse to melt, hiding in shady spots like winter's final soldiers.
Frogs and toads typically start their loud, croaky concerts around this date as they wake up from winter hibernation and look for mates.
